content.text: can you review the faq changes in this branch, can you look at them as an engineer being familiar with general usage of git and how repositories work

I’m reviewing the FAQ changes in this branch with a code-review lens, focusing on technical correctness against how Git and repositories actually work. First I’m locating the changed files and reading the diff and surrounding context.
I found the FAQ in `README.md`; next I’m reading the actual edits and comparing them to the base branch so I can review the content, not just the last commit.
I’ve confirmed the branch is only touching `README.md`. Now I’m diffing it against `main` and then checking the implementation/docs for any technical inaccuracies in the new FAQ answers.

input.cmd: rg -n "shallow|partial|filter|force|prune|replicate|relay|receive-pack|atomic|SSH|smart HTTP|https|daemon|background watcher|bootstrap|temporary refs|resume" README.md docs internal cmd pkg . -S

content.text: I’m checking a few claims that look easy to overstate in Git terms, especially “complete history” and “atomic.” Those are the places docs usually drift from what the implementation and Git servers actually guarantee.

input.cmd: rg -n "atomic|atomicity|atomic push|push atomic|capability.*atomic|\batomic\b" internal cmd docs README.md -S
rg -n "include-tag|tags needed|annotated tag|tag.*needed|selected refs|managed refs|full objects needed|complete history|required for the selected refs" internal docs README.md -S
rg -n "report-status|ng |unpack ok|ok |partial success|accepted|rejected|receive-pack report|PushReport|status report" internal -S

content.text: The main concrete problem I’ve found is the atomicity answer: the implementation does not request Git’s `atomic` push capability, so a multi-ref push can succeed for some refs and fail for others. I’m verifying whether there are any other factual mismatches of that kind before I wrap up.

input.cmd: rg -n "supports branch refs and create-only tags|create-only tags|batched bootstrap currently supports branch refs and create-only tags|temporary ref|TempRefs|resume" internal/strategy/bootstrap internal/syncer docs README.md -S
rg -n "http://|https://|HTTP/HTTPS|smart HTTP/HTTPS only|smart-HTTP-only|HTTPS-only" README.md docs internal -S

content.text:
1. High: [README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/README.md:115) overstates atomicity. The answer says “A single target `receive-pack` push is atomic in the normal Git sense,” but the implementation never negotiates Git’s `atomic` push capability; it only sets sideband, `report-status`, and `delete-refs` when needed ([internal/gitproto/push.go](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/internal/gitproto/push.go:61)). In Git terms that means a multi-ref push can partially succeed: some refs may update and others may be rejected, with `report-status` merely reporting that afterward ([internal/gitproto/push.go](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/internal/gitproto/push.go:136)). I’d reword this to say the tool pre-validates unsafe updates, but target-side partial success is still possible within a single push.

2. Low: the new SSH answer in [README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/README.md:119) says “smart HTTP/HTTPS only,” which is technically consistent with the codebase, but it now conflicts with existing docs that still say “HTTPS-only” ([docs/protocol.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/docs/protocol.md:23)) while also elsewhere calling the transport “smart-HTTP-only” ([docs/protocol.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/docs/protocol.md:37)). Since this FAQ will become the fast path users read first, I’d align those docs in the same branch to avoid transport confusion.

I didn’t find other obvious Git-semantics problems in the FAQ. The rest reads broadly consistent with how refs, reachable objects, one-way sync, prune, and relay/materialized paths work here.
ok, let's remove atomic from the README.md and then maybe remove the faq item?

I’m removing the atomicity FAQ entry entirely rather than softening it, since the current answer is the misleading part and the rest of the FAQ stands on its own. After the edit I’ll show the resulting diff.

input.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/README.md
@@
-### How does it deal with partial successes? Is it atomic?
-
-`git-sync` plans before pushing and blocks unsafe refs before starting. A single target `receive-pack` push is atomic in the normal Git sense for that push. Across multiple batches or separate push requests, especially batched bootstrap, it is not globally atomic; batched bootstrap uses temporary refs and resume behavior.
-
 ### Does it support SSH?
*** End Patch

content.text: The FAQ entry is out. I’m checking the diff now to confirm only that section changed.

Output:
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 973b3812..960865a5 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -110,10 +110,6 @@ The scalable path streams pack data from the source directly into the target wit
 
 There is no fixed runtime. It depends on repository size, pack size, network latency, provider speed, and whether relay is used. On the relay path, time is mostly source pack generation plus network transfer plus target receive time.
 
-### How does it deal with partial successes? Is it atomic?
-
-`git-sync` plans before pushing and blocks unsafe refs before starting. A single target `receive-pack` push is atomic in the normal Git sense for that push. Across multiple batches or separate push requests, especially batched bootstrap, it is not globally atomic; batched bootstrap uses temporary refs and resume behavior.
-
 ### Does it support SSH?
 
 No. `git-sync` supports smart HTTP/HTTPS only.

content.text: I removed the atomicity FAQ item from [README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/gitsync/README.md:110). The diff is limited to deleting that question and answer; no other FAQ content changed.
